title: G Major Scale Run
instruction: Play the G major scale up and down the neck for 2 minutes. Focus on clean, even notes.

title: Alternate Picking Drill
instruction: Practice alternate picking on a single string at 80 BPM. Keep each note even and consistent.

title: Chromatic Exercise
instruction: Play a chromatic exercise (1-2-3-4 pattern) across all 6 strings, ascending and descending.

title: Hammer-Ons & Pull-Offs
instruction: Practice hammer-ons and pull-offs on the high E string. Focus on clarity — each note should ring cleanly.

title: Chord Transition Drill
instruction: Cycle through C, G, D, and Em chord transitions. Aim for a clean switch with no buzzing.

title: Metronome Strumming
instruction: Strum a basic chord progression along to a metronome at 70 BPM. Focus purely on locking in with the beat.

title: Down-Up Strumming
instruction: Practice a consistent down-up strumming pattern for 2 minutes. Keep your wrist loose and the rhythm even.

title: Clap the Rhythm
instruction: Set the metronome to 80 BPM. Clap or mute-strum to match the beat precisely for 1 minute. Then double the subdivisions.

title: BPM Climb
instruction: Start at 60 BPM. Every 30 seconds, increase by 5 BPM. See how high you can go while keeping clean strums.

title: Sync Chord Changes
instruction: Change chords precisely on the beat. Use Em to Am. Every chord change must land exactly on beat 1.

title: Learn a New Chord
instruction: Look up a chord you've never played before. Learn its shape, strum it slowly, and try to memorize the fingering.

title: Barre Chord Practice
instruction: Practice F and Bm barre chords. Press firmly and strum — each string must ring clearly.

title: I–V–vi–IV Progression
instruction: Play the I–V–vi–IV progression in the key of G: G, D, Em, C. Repeat and keep time.

title: Open to Barre Switch
instruction: Alternate between an open G chord and a Bm barre chord. Focus on fast, clean transitions.

title: Replicate a Melody
instruction: Hum or recall a simple melody (like Happy Birthday). Try to find and play each note on your guitar by ear.

title: Tune by Ear
instruction: Without a tuner, try to tune your guitar to itself: fret the 5th fret and match it to the open string above.

title: Major vs. Minor
instruction: Play a major chord, then its minor version (e.g., A major then A minor). Notice the difference. Repeat for 3 different roots.

title: E Minor Improvisation
instruction: Improvise freely for 2 minutes in the key of E minor. Don't overthink it — just play what feels right.

title: Write a 4-Chord Progression
instruction: Create your own 4-chord progression using any chords you know. Play it until it sounds musical.

title: 3-Note Riff
instruction: Invent a riff using only 3 notes. Repeat it with rhythm variations — try fast, slow, syncopated.
